prompt: Create an elegant vertical Art Nouveau poster for SOLARA, a radiant sun goddess standing front-facing in a graceful symmetrical pose. She has long flowing auburn-brown hair streaming outward, crowned with a lavish floral wreath and golden ornaments, with a glowing circular sun halo behind her head and large stylized golden sun rays filling the upper background. Her face is intentionally obscured by a plain vertical rectangle in warm brown, centered over the face area. She wears a deep crimson and burgundy Greco-Roman draped dress with gold floral patterning, thin straps, and ornate gold chains; a large sun medallion sits at her chest with delicate dangling chains. Add gold jewelry including bracelets, arm bands, a necklace, and small ornamental medallions. Her arms extend outward as she lightly holds the flowing fabric of her dress and scarf-like sleeves. The background is rich dark red and burnt orange with luxurious antique gold linework, swirling ornamental curves, vines, and floral motifs. Include exactly two large sunflower arrangements in the top left and top right corners, plus one sunflower motif near the lower right corner, with additional smaller leaves and vine details throughout. Frame the entire poster with a thin decorative gold border and rounded Art Nouveau corner ornaments. At the bottom, place a dark red framed title plaque with ornate gold trim. Text on the plaque: large serif title "SOLARA"; below it smaller French subtitle "DÉESSE DU SOLEIL"; below that two centered lines in small gold capitals: "SOURCE DE VIE • LUMIÈRE ÉTERNELLE" and "FLAMME DU JOUR • CHALEUR SACRÉE"; at the very bottom center write "PARIS" with a tiny gold floral divider. Use a refined European vintage poster style inspired by Alphonse Mucha, with flat decorative illustration, intricate gold detailing, aged paper grain, muted crimson, ochre, amber, and antique gold palette, slightly distressed print texture, and no modern elements.
